definition: Traditional leading indicators including yield curve inversion, Conference Board LEI, ISM PMI, and the Sahm Rule have exhibited degraded predictive power in the post-COVID regime, with the yield curve inverted 22-24 months without recession, LEI declining 40+ consecutive months without recession, and the Sahm Rule triggering in July 2024 without recession.
evidence: Yield curve inverted mid-2022, remained inverted 22-24 months — longest without recession in modern history
LEI declined 40+ consecutive months without recession — unprecedented in 60-year history
Sahm Rule triggered July 2024 without recession
ISM Manufacturing PMI spent extended periods below 50 while GDP grew above trend
All four debates confirmed the factual record as unambiguous
open_questions: Are indicators temporarily overridden by fiscal or permanently degraded by structural economic shifts?
Will indicators reassert when fiscal impulse contracts?
Is the degradation caused by services-dominant economy and manufacturing-centric indicator design?
